remember: Students will be able to recall and describe the characteristics of amorphous and crystalline solids.
Students will be able to differentiate between amorphous and crystalline solids based on their properties.
Students will be able to list and describe the types of crystal lattices.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of unit cell and its types.
Students will be able to understand the relationship between crystal lattice and unit cell.
Students will be able to describe the different types of crystal defects and their consequences.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the number of atoms in a unit cell using the formulae for different types of unit cells.
Students will be able to determine the type of crystal lattice based on the given crystal structure.
Students will be able to explain the effect of crystal defects on the properties of solids.
analyze: Students will be able to compare the properties of amorphous and crystalline solids.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between crystal lattice and the physical properties of solids.
Students will be able to evaluate the effect of crystal defects on the optical and electrical properties of solids.

ncert_objectives: Explain the characteristics of amorphous and crystalline solids.
Distinguish between amorphous and crystalline solids.
Describe the types of crystal lattices and unit cells.
Explain the relationship between crystal lattice and unit cell.
Describe the different types of crystal defects and their consequences.
board_exam_objectives: Recall and describe the characteristics of amorphous and crystalline solids.
Determine the type of crystal lattice based on the given crystal structure.
Explain the effect of crystal defects on the properties of solids.
Compare the properties of amorphous and crystalline solids.
